factors.summary: Demand for ADT's offerings is influenced by economic conditions, competition, housing market trends, and customer perceptions. The company expects demand to be affected by factors such as DIY market growth and macroeconomic conditions.

evidence.text: Demand for our offerings may be impacted by... (i) overall economic conditions... (ii) type, price and quality of our offerings compared to those of our competitors; (iii) changes in competition... (iv) overall state of the housing market; (v) perceived threat of crime; (vi) occurrence of significant life events... and (vii) availability of financial incentives provided by insurance carriers.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly with the transition to the ADT+ platform, which has increased the proportion of outright sales and professional installations.

evidence.text: As we continue to build our partnership with Google... we expect to continue to see a shift toward an increasing proportion of outright sales transactions... which will impact results in future periods when those changes occur.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflationary pressures, but the company is managing them through price increases and cost-saving initiatives.

evidence.text: We have made significant progress toward increasing the variety of our offerings... For example, our Remote Assistance Program... (v) inflationary pressures on costs such as materials, labor, and fuel; and (vi) other changes in prices, interest rates, or terms from our suppliers or vendors, or third party lenders. We aim to continuously evaluate and respond to changes in the above to drive efficiencies and meet customer demands.

factors.summary: A key near-term risk is the successful closing of the new $600 million first lien term loan facility by March 7, 2025, which is subject to market and customary conditions.

evidence.text: The closing of this new facility remains subject to market and other customary conditions. The company intends to use proceeds for partial redemption of the First Lien Notes due 2026 and other general corporate purposes.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is focused on optimizing its capital structure and managing debt effectively to support future growth.

evidence.text: The company has been actively managing its debt through refinancing and amendments to its credit agreements, which are part of its long-term strategy to maintain financial stability and support growth.

factors.summary: A key catalyst is the expected closing of the new $600 million first lien term loan facility, which will provide funds for debt redemption and general corporate purposes.

evidence.text: The closing of this new facility is expected to occur on or around March 7, 2025, with proceeds to be used for the partial redemption of the First Lien Notes due 2026 and other general corporate purposes.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable and supported by comprehensive programs that ensure employee satisfaction and engagement.

evidence.text: Our comprehensive employee listening program collects feedback and sentiment at every stage of the employee experience, including an annual sentiment survey. The annual sentiment survey was completed by approximately two thirds of employees, up from the prior year, and showed favorable results for most topics.

factors.summary: The company is in a growth mode, actively managing its workforce to support talent attraction and retention.

evidence.text: We continue to deploy talent using a mix of hybrid, remote, and in-person work arrangements, based on the nature of the role, to support talent attraction and retention in alignment with business needs.

factors.summary: Employee well-being is a significant focus with comprehensive wellness programs and initiatives in place.

evidence.text: We devote significant resources to employees’ wellness, health, and safety. We continue to provide to all employees ADT Balance, which is an annual well-being program that offers a robust variety of features, including biometric screenings, team fitness challenges, webinars, group coaching, and self-guided resources.

factors.summary: The company is dependent on a few large customers, increasing customer concentration risk.

evidence.text: Qualifying customers can pay any upfront fees over the course of the contract (referred to as retail installment contracts). A security interest is granted in the retail installment contract receivables as collateral for cash borrowings under our uncommitted receivables securitization financing agreement (the “2020 Receivables Facility”)

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages, particularly its brand names, are under threat from unauthorized use by third parties.

evidence.text: Our brand names are critical to our success. Unauthorized use of our brand names by third parties may materially adversely affect our business and reputation, including the perceived quality and reliability of our products and services, as well as brand loyalty.
evidence.subsection: Unauthorized use of our brand names by third parties, and the expenses incurred in developing and preserving the value of our brand names, may materially adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: The company faces risks related to market share due to potential unauthorized use of its brand names and potential legal challenges.

evidence.text: Unauthorized use of our brand names by third parties may... impact our brands. We may not be successful in detecting, investigating, preventing, or prosecuting all unauthorized third-party use of our brand names.
evidence.subsection: Unauthorized use of our brand names by third parties, and the expenses incurred in developing and preserving the value of our brand names, may materially adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: The company is increasing its capital expenditures as it transitions its technology infrastructure to the cloud, which is a significant initiative requiring substantial investment.

evidence.text: As we transition our technology infrastructure to the cloud, we may need to divert resources, including management attention, away from other important business operations.
evidence.subsection: Delays, costs, and disruptions that result from upgrading, integrating, and maintaining the security of our information and technology networks and systems could materially adversely affect us.

factors.summary: The company is exposed to significant cybersecurity risks, including attacks that could disrupt operations and compromise sensitive information.

evidence.text: Cybersecurity attacks or threats or other unauthorized access or attempts to access to our systems, or those of third parties, have in the past, and may in the future, compromise the security of our systems and otherwise disrupt our normal operations.
evidence.subsection: Delays, costs, and disruptions that result from upgrading, integrating, and maintaining the security of our information and technology networks and systems could materially adversely affect us.

factors.summary: The company's supply chain is still disrupted due to the migration to cloud infrastructure and reliance on third-party cloud services, which may cause delays and increased costs.

evidence.text: As we transition our technology infrastructure to the cloud, we may need to divert resources, including management attention, away from other important business operations.
evidence.subsection: Delays, costs, and disruptions that result from upgrading, integrating, and maintaining the security of our information and technology networks and systems could materially adversely affect us.

evidence.text: Continuing expansion in customers’ options to choose systems that they can in part or fully install could increase our attrition rates over time and the risk of accelerated amortization of customer contracts resulting from a declining customer base.

factors.summary: New product launches, such as ADT+, are facing challenges in timely and successful commercialization.

evidence.text: We may not achieve a successful ongoing rollout of the platform, and new platform features, in a timely manner, within budget, or in a manner that enables the commercialization of products and services that meet the continually evolving demands of our customers, any of which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Risks Related to Our Products and Services

factors.summary: Labor costs and wage inflation could create margin pressure due to a competitive labor environment.

evidence.text: Our success could be impacted adversely by the competitive labor environment and require us to incur wages and benefits in excess of our planned expenditure.
evidence.subsection: If we are unable to recruit and retain sufficient personnel at all levels of our organization, our ability to manage our business could be materially adversely affected.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations pose compliance costs and operational constraints.

evidence.text: Changes in environmental laws or regulations could impose significant operational restrictions and compliance requirements upon our business or products...
evidence.subsection: Increasing government regulation of telemarketing, email marketing, door-to-door sales, and other marketing methods may increase our costs and restrict the operation and growth of our business.

factors.summary: The company has authorized a share repurchase program, which could indicate management confidence in the stock.

evidence.text: On February 20, 2025, our Board of Directors authorized a share repurchase plan (the “2025 Share Repurchase Plan”), pursuant to which the Company is authorized to repurchase, through April 30, 2026, up to a maximum aggregate amount of $500 million of shares of the Company's Common Stock.
evidence.subsection: We may issue preferred securities, the terms of which could adversely affect the voting power or value of our Common Stock.

factors.summary: The company is affected by tariffs and trade restrictions, leading to increased costs and potential supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: Existing or new tariffs and other trade restrictions imposed on imports from China, Mexico, or other countries where much of our end-user equipment is manufactured, or any counter-measures taken in response, may harm our business and results of operations. For example, in 2018 and 2019, the U.S. federal government imposed tariffs on certain alarm equipment components manufactured in China, and on other categories of electronic equipment manufactured in China that we install in our customers’ premises, such as batteries and thermostats.
evidence.subsection: Our business would be adversely affected if certain of our independent contractors were classified as employees.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving across the company, driven by effective management of credit facilities, successful debt refinancing, and strategic divestitures that have optimized the capital structure and reduced debt.

evidence.text: The increase in net cash provided by operating activities for 2024 compared to 2023 was primarily due to a decrease in cash interest of $149 million primarily related to reduced principal balances on our debt, the benefit of lower outflows in the current year related to our former Solar Business, and improved operating performance.

Proceeds from the First Lien Revolving Credit Facility and other financing activities have been utilized to redeem high-cost debt and optimize the capital structure, leading to improved cash flow generation.

The Commercial Divestiture supported our strategic vision and strengthened our financial profile, and we used net proceeds of approximately $1,518 million to reduce our debt. Refer to Note 7 “Debt” in the Notes to Consolidated Financial Statements in Item 15 “Exhibit and Financial Statement Schedules” for further information.

factors.summary: The company is actively managing its debt by deleveraging and refinancing at lower rates, which has reduced its interest expense and strengthened its financial profile. However, the company still has a significant amount of outstanding indebtedness, which could limit its financial flexibility and increase its vulnerability to economic downturns.

evidence.text: During 2024, the decrease in interest expense, net, as compared to the prior year period, was primarily driven by lower interest expense of $114 million primarily related to lower principal amounts outstanding on our First Lien Term Loan B and the redemption of our First Lien Notes due 2024.

In October 2023, we redeemed approximately $1.3 billion of the First Lien Term Loan B due 2026 using net proceeds from the Commercial Divestiture. In the same month, we amended and restated the First Lien Credit Agreement to refinance the remaining outstanding balance with a new $1,375 million 7-year term First Lien Term Loan B due 2030.
The Commercial Divestiture supported our strategic vision and strengthened our financial profile, and we used net proceeds of approximately $1,518 million to reduce our debt.

As of December 31, 2024, we had $7.8 billion face value of outstanding indebtedness, excluding finance leases, and we may increase our debt level at any time. Such substantial indebtedness negatively impacts our business because...

factors.summary: The company's organic revenue growth is showing mixed trends. While some areas indicate stable or positive growth driven by higher average prices, increased installation revenue, and new product launches, there are signs of deceleration and challenges due to competitive pressures and market dynamics. Additionally, the impact of the ADT+ platform rollout and other strategic initiatives is contributing to these varying trends.

evidence.text: The increase in other income (expense) was primarily due to higher income from the Commercial TSA of $28 million, with the remainder of the change primarily due to the realized gain (loss) on interest rate swaps.

During 2022, as part of our partnership with Google, we launched the Google Nest doorbell, rolled out mesh Wi-Fi, and launched Google indoor and outdoor cameras as part of our product offerings. In the first quarter of 2023, we launched our proprietary ADT+ app for our self set-up line of DIY smart home security products, including Google Nest offerings, which allows customers to easily access and control their ADT devices through an intuitive app experience.

During the fourth quarter of 2023, we began our phased rollout of our ADT+ app for professional installation along with a new interactive and hardware lineup. During 2024, the Company continued its phased rollout of the ADT+ platform across the country.

The home security and automation markets in which we sell our products and services are highly competitive, which may result in pressure on our profit margins and limit our ability to maintain or increase the market share of our products and services.

While we have experienced some increase in costs as a result of inflation, we have, for the most part, been able to offset the rising costs through price increases to our customers, as well as cost-saving opportunities.

The failure of our technology, products, or services to gain market acceptance, the potential for product defects, or the obsolescence of our products and services could significantly reduce our revenue, increase our operating costs, or otherwise materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

factors.summary: The company is enhancing productivity through multiple initiatives, including operational restructuring, digital tools, and remote services. These efforts aim to reduce costs, minimize vehicle trips, and streamline operations.

evidence.text: In November 2023, we announced a plan to streamline the Solar Business to focus on the top performing markets and rationalize the overhead and infrastructure of the business. For example, our Remote Assistance Program provides our customers the ability to troubleshoot and resolve certain service issues, as well as other functions, through a live video stream with our skilled technicians. This provides customers with more options for receiving certain services that best fit their lifestyles while reducing our costs and eliminating thousands of vehicle trips.

Our innovative remote assistance program (the “Remote Assistance Program”) delivers a scalable, cost-efficient means of servicing our customers through live video streaming with our skilled technicians to troubleshoot and resolve service issues as well as remote programming and installation support for new DIY systems, add-ons, and resale reactivations. We are also exploring and implementing AI tools that are aimed to improve and automate certain processes for our call center agents and customers.

factors.summary: The company has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in its ability to meet expectations through ongoing refinancing and cost management. However, there are no specific mentions of changes in expectations or potential risks affecting forward guidance.

evidence.text: The company has consistently met its financial obligations and maintained its credit agreements, signaling confidence in its ability to adhere to forward guidance.

We cannot guarantee that our share repurchase program will be fully consummated or that it will enhance long-term shareholder value. Share repurchases and dividend payments, including recent changes in the amount of our dividend, could also increase the volatility of the trading price of our stock and will diminish our cash reserves.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated active M&A and strategic alliance activities, including significant divestitures and partnerships, which have both positive and negative implications. However, these activities also pose risks, such as challenges in integrating systems, potential brand damage, and competition in acquiring new accounts.

evidence.text: In January 2024, after a strategic review of the business and continued macroeconomic and industry pressures, the Company’s board of directors (the “Board of Directors”) approved a plan to fully exit the residential Solar Business; and as of June 30, 2024, substantially all operations of the Solar Business had ceased.

On October 2, 2023, we completed the Commercial Divestiture for a total purchase price of approximately $1,613 million in cash, subject to customary post-closing adjustments, and received net proceeds of approximately $1,585 million, excluding transaction costs.

In July 2020, we entered into a Master Supply, Distribution, and Marketing Agreement with Google (as amended, the “Google Commercial Agreement”) pursuant to which Google has agreed to supply us with certain Google devices as well as certain Google video and analytics services (the “Google Devices and Services”) for sale to our customers.

In September 2022, we entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of September 5, 2022, with State Farm (the “State Farm Securities Purchase Agreement”), pursuant to which we agreed to issue and sell in a private placement to State Farm, 133,333,333 shares of our Common Stock (the “State Farm Shares”) at a per share price of $9.00 for an aggregate purchase price of $1.2 billion (the “State Farm Strategic Investment”)
We have completed a significant number of acquisitions of companies that operate different technology platforms and systems. We are also integrating systems from our various acquisitions.

factors.summary: Economic conditions pose significant risks to the company, primarily as a result of inflation, macroeconomic uncertainty, and housing market trends, which negatively impact customer behavior and demand. Additionally, the company faces challenges related to high debt levels and refinancing risks, though it is actively managing its debt to mitigate these impacts. There are also risks associated with changes in the insurance industry's incentives for alarm monitoring services, which could further reduce demand and increase customer attrition. Overall, the economic environment is a notable headwind, with multiple factors contributing to potential growth inhibition and operational challenges.

evidence.text: Demand for our offerings may be impacted by... (i) overall economic conditions in the geographies in which we operate;... Relocations are sensitive to changes in the residential housing market, and fewer relocations generally lead to improvements in gross customer revenue attrition but fewer new customer additions. Additionally, non-payment disconnects generally increase in a weaker macroeconomic environment.
The company is managing its debt through refinancing and amendments to its credit agreements, which is helping to mitigate the impact of economic headwinds.

If the insurance industry changes its practice of providing incentives to homeowners for the use of alarm monitoring services, we may experience a reduction in new customer growth or an increase in our customer attrition rate.
evidence.subsection: Risks Related to Our Products and Services

evidence.text: General economic conditions can affect our business, and we are susceptible to changes in the business economy, in the housing market, and in business and consumer discretionary income, which may inhibit our ability to grow our customer base and impact our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Risks Related to Macroeconomic and Related Factors

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, as evidenced by successful price increases contributing to revenue growth and cost management. However, this is tempered by competitive pressures and potential regulatory risks that could undermine pricing effectiveness.

evidence.text: The increase in RMR, as compared to the prior period, was primarily driven by an increase in average prices on new and existing subscribers. The prices we are able to charge for our products and services are impacted by... and the microeconomic and macroeconomic environments in which we operate.

Competitors may offer superior products or business strategies, impairing our brand and the desirability of our products and services, which may cause customers to defer or forego purchases of our products and services, or to decline to enter into new monthly installment contracts or to cancel or fail to renew existing contracts.

evidence.text: Consumer protection policies or legal precedents could be proposed or adopted to restrict the charges we can impose upon contract cancellation. Such initiatives could compel us to increase our prices during the initial term of our contracts and consequently lead to less demand for our services and increased customer attrition.
evidence.subsection: Adoption of statutes and governmental policies purporting to characterize certain of our charges as unlawful may adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident through active debt management, share repurchase programs, and maintaining compliance with financial covenants. However, the potential issuance of preferred securities could impact the value and voting power of common stock, introducing some uncertainty.

evidence.text: The company has successfully refinanced its debt at lower rates and maintained compliance with all financial covenants, demonstrating management's confidence in the company's financial stability.

The 2025 Share Repurchase Plan allows the Company to purchase shares of its Common Stock from time to time in one or more open market or privately negotiated transactions, including pursuant to Rule 10b5-1 or Rule 10b-18 of the Exchange Act or pursuant to one or more accelerated share repurchase agreements, subject to certain requirements and other factors.
evidence.subsection: We may issue preferred securities, the terms of which could adversely affect the voting power or value of our Common Stock.

factors.summary: The company faces significant sensitivities related to both financial and operational factors. On one hand, interest rates and debt service costs are critical near-term factors impacting the company's financial health. On the other hand, geopolitical risks such as natural disasters, pandemics, political instability, and economic downturns pose substantial threats to the company's operations and reputation. These dual challenges underscore the company's vulnerability to both external economic conditions and global stability issues.

evidence.text: The company is subject to interest rate changes, which affect its borrowing costs. Management has been actively managing its debt to mitigate the impact of potential rate changes.

The occurrence of natural disasters, pandemics, political or economic instability, or other activities in such countries could result in the sudden and continued closure of operations that in turn could cause disruptions in our operations...
evidence.subsection: Our reputation as a service provider of high-quality security offerings may be materially adversely affected by product defects or shortfalls in customer service.

factors.summary: The impact of interest rates on the company is mixed. While the company has successfully reduced borrowing costs through refinancing at lower rates, rising interest rates have negatively affected financing costs and profitability.

evidence.text: The company has refinanced its debt at lower rates, such as reducing the interest rate on the First Lien Term Loan B due 2030 from Term SOFR +2.50% to Term SOFR +2.00% over time.

Rising interest rates, as we have experienced during recent years and which we may continue to experience, may increase the lenders’ cost of capital and those increased costs will result in an increase in the fees charged to us. In addition, where we have committed to provide financing internally, as interest rates rise, our cost of capital also gets more expensive and we may not be able to pass on such increased costs to our customers.
evidence.subsection: Rising interest rates or increased consumer lender fees could adversely impact our sales, profitability, and our financing costs.

factors.summary: The company is facing mixed challenges in talent retention, with some evidence indicating successful programs and policies for attracting and retaining top talent, particularly through long-term equity compensation plans. However, other factors, such as difficulties in retaining sufficiently skilled personnel for new systems and the impact of a competitive labor market and potential loss of key personnel, pose risks to talent retention.

evidence.text: Our success in attracting, retaining, and developing a strong, dedicated, and diverse workforce largely depends on the hiring and retaining of top talent across the entire organization... In addition, our long-term equity compensation plans are intended to align management interests with those of our stockholders and to encourage the creation of long-term value.

evidence.subsection: If we are unable to recruit and retain sufficient personnel at all levels of our organization, our ability to manage our business could be materially adversely affected.

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks from various regulatory changes, including increased scrutiny over foreign labor practices and anti-corruption laws, rising compliance costs due to evolving privacy and data security regulations, potential restrictions on marketing methods such as telemarketing and email marketing, impacts on customer acquisition and retention from subscription service regulations, and challenges related to net neutrality and labor laws. These changes could increase operational costs, restrict business operations, and affect financial stability.

evidence.text: The practice of utilizing labor based in foreign countries has come under increased scrutiny in the United States. Governmental authorities could seek to limit or penalize our operations...

evidence.text: The FTC’s “Click-to-Cancel rule... and certain proposed state regulations concerning subscription services may require ADT to provide additional notices to consumers... which may have a material adverse effect on ADT’s ability to attract and retain customers.
evidence.subsection: Increasing government regulation of telemarketing, email marketing, door-to-door sales, and other marketing methods may increase our costs and restrict the operation and growth of our business.

evidence.text: In March 2015, the FCC released net neutrality rules prohibiting broadband ISPs from blocking, throttling, or engaging in “paid prioritization” of content or services. However, these rules were repealed in 2017. As of April 2024, the FCC reinstated net neutrality regulations, effectively reversing the 2017 repeal, once again classifying broadband as a telecommunications service under Title II of the Federal Communications Act of 1934, as amended. However, in June 2024, the U.S. Supreme Court overruled the doctrine known as Chevron Deference, which previously required courts to defer to an agency’s reasonable interpretation of law when the law was ambiguous. As a result, on January 2, 2025, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit struck down the FCC’s previously adopted Safeguarding and Securing the Open Internet rule (“SSOI”), ruling that the FCC incorrectly classified ISPs as telecommunications service providers, rather than non-common carrier providers of information services, and therefore exceeded its authority in imposing the net neutrality regulations.
evidence.subsection: Adoption of statutes and governmental policies purporting to characterize certain of our charges as unlawful may adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation risks from various sources, including intellectual property infringement claims, unauthorized use of brand names, non-competition agreements, and potential class action lawsuits related to consumer protection, privacy, and regulatory compliance. Additionally, the company may incur substantial legal costs and face reputational harm due to these risks.

evidence.text: In addition, the Company and Iris Buyer agreed to customary employee non-solicitation and no-hire restrictions with respect to the other party’s employees for a period of two years from the closing. The Company and Iris Buyer also agreed to a non-competition period of five years from the closing whereby, subject to certain exceptions, (a) ADT and its subsidiaries will not directly or indirectly sell or service in the United States (or any of its territories): (i) security, building automation, access control, retail store performance, electronic article surveillance, or cash processing/ATM security equipment or services for non-residential premises over 10,000 square feet, (ii) nurse call solutions, or (iii) fire detection or fire suppression products or services for non-residential premises of any size; and (b) F&S Holdings and its subsidiaries will not directly or indirectly sell or service in the United States (or any of its territories) or Canada, among other items: (i) security, building automation or access control products or services for any non-residential premises under 10,000 square feet or any residential premises, (ii) personal emergency response systems or mobile safety solutions (other than nurse call solutions), or (iii) fire detection products or services for residential premises.
Future litigation with respect to such unauthorized use could also result in substantial costs and diversion of our resources.

evidence.text: We have been, and in the future may become, the target of additional lawsuits alleging that we have infringed the patents or technology of third parties. Regardless of the merits of these lawsuits and any steps we take to mitigate infringement risk, any allegations could cause us to incur significant costs to defend and resolve, and could harm our business and reputation, any of which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.

evidence.text: Adverse judicial determinations regarding these matters could cause us to incur legal exposure to customers against whom such charges have been imposed and expose us to the risk that certain of our customers may seek to recover such charges through litigation, including class action lawsuits. Any such loss in demand for our services, increase in attrition, or the costs of defending such litigation and enforcement actions could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Adoption of statutes and governmental policies purporting to characterize certain of our charges as unlawful may adversely affect our business.
